civil.definition: relating to ordinary citizens; polite and courteous

coalition.definition: an alliance formed for combined action

collective.definition: done by people acting as a group; a cooperative enterprise

colonialism.definition: the practice of acquiring political control over another country

commodity.definition: a raw material or agricultural product; something useful

communism.definition: a political theory based on common ownership of production

constitution.definition: a body of fundamental principles; the basic law of a nation

corruption.definition: dishonest conduct by those in power; decay or putrefaction

creditor.definition: a person or company to whom money is owed

currency.definition: a system of money in general use in a particular country

deficit.definition: the amount by which spending exceeds income; a shortfall

delegate.definition: to entrust a task to another person; a person sent to represent others

democracy.definition: a system of government where citizens vote for representatives

despot.definition: a ruler who holds absolute power and uses it oppressively

diaspora.definition: people who spread from their homeland to other countries

diplomacy.definition: the profession of managing international relations

discrimination.definition: the unjust treatment of different categories of people

diversity.definition: the state of being diverse; a range of different things

dividend.definition: a sum paid to shareholders from a company's profits

economy.definition: the state of a country in terms of production and consumption

election.definition: a formal process of choosing a person for a public position by voting

embargo.definition: an official ban on trade with a particular country

emigrate.definition: to leave one's own country to settle in another

empathy.definition: the ability to understand and share the feelings of another

equity.definition: the quality of being fair; stocks and shares in a company

ethnicity.definition: the fact of belonging to a particular ethnic group

fascism.definition: an authoritarian and nationalistic system of government

federalism.definition: a system of government in which power is divided between central and regional authorities

feudalism.definition: the medieval system of holding land in exchange for military service

fiscal.definition: relating to government revenue through taxes

franchise.definition: an authorization to sell or distribute goods; the right to vote

gender.definition: the state of being male, female, or another identity

globalization.definition: the process by which businesses become international in scope

governance.definition: the action of governing; the manner in which an organization is controlled

grassroots.definition: relating to ordinary people as the basis of a political movement

gross domestic product.definition: the total value of goods produced and services provided in a country

ideology.definition: a system of ideas and ideals forming the basis of a theory or policy

immigration.definition: the action of coming to live permanently in a foreign country

imperialism.definition: a policy of extending power through colonization

incentive.definition: a thing that motivates or encourages someone to do something

inequality.definition: difference in size, degree, circumstances, or rights

inflation.definition: a general increase in prices and fall in purchasing power

institution.definition: an established organization with a particular purpose

integration.definition: the action of combining parts into a whole; racial desegregation

interest rate.definition: the amount charged for borrowing money, expressed as a percentage

jurisdiction.definition: the official power to make legal decisions

justice.definition: just behavior or treatment; the quality of being fair and reasonable

legislation.definition: laws collectively; the process of making laws

liberal.definition: open to new behavior or opinions; favoring individual rights

lobbyist.definition: a person who tries to influence legislation on behalf of a group

manifesto.definition: a public declaration of policy and aims

marginalize.definition: to treat a person or group as insignificant or peripheral

monarchy.definition: a form of government with a monarch at the head

monopoly.definition: exclusive control of a commodity or service by one company

nationalism.definition: identification with one's nation and support of its interests

oligarchy.definition: a small group exercising control in a country

philanthropy.definition: the desire to promote welfare of others; charitable acts

policy.definition: a course of action adopted by a government or organization

poverty.definition: the state of being extremely poor; the lack of necessities

recession.definition: a period of temporary economic decline

referendum.definition: a general vote on a single political question

refugee.definition: a person who has been forced to leave their country

regulation.definition: a rule or directive made and maintained by an authority

republic.definition: a state in which supreme power is held by the people

revenue.definition: income generated from normal business operations

sanction.definition: a penalty for disobeying a rule; official permission

secularism.definition: the principle of separation of government from religious institutions

segregation.definition: the action of setting apart; enforced separation by race

socialism.definition: a political and economic theory of collective ownership

sovereignty.definition: supreme power held by a state; self-governing status

stereotype.definition: a widely held oversimplified image of a type of person

subsidy.definition: a sum of money granted by the government to assist an industry

suffrage.definition: the right to vote in political elections

surplus.definition: an amount left over when requirements have been met; an excess

tariff.definition: a tax on imports or exports

taxation.definition: the levying of tax; imposing a tax on someone

totalitarian.definition: relating to a system requiring complete subservience to the state

trade.definition: the buying and selling of goods and services

transparency.definition: the condition of being transparent; openness and accountability

treaty.definition: a formally concluded and ratified agreement between countries

tribunal.definition: a court of justice; a body established to settle certain disputes

tyranny.definition: cruel and oppressive government or rule

unemployment.definition: the state of being without a paid job while seeking work

veto.definition: a right to reject a decision; to exercise such a right

welfare.definition: the health and happiness of a person; financial support from the state

allegory.definition: a story representing a deeper meaning through symbolic fiction

alliteration.definition: the occurrence of the same letter at the beginning of adjacent words

anachronism.definition: a thing belonging to a period other than the one it exists in

anthology.definition: a collection of works by various authors

architecture.definition: the art of designing and constructing buildings

autobiography.definition: an account of one's life written by oneself

ballad.definition: a poem or song narrating a story in short stanzas

baroque.definition: of an extravagant ornamental style of art and music

biography.definition: a detailed description of a person's life

bronze.definition: a yellowish-brown alloy of copper and tin; a brownish color

caricature.definition: a picture or description exaggerating features for comic effect

ceramic.definition: relating to pots and other articles made from clay

choreography.definition: the art of composing sequences of steps and moves for dance

cinematography.definition: the art of photography in filmmaking

collage.definition: a piece of art made by sticking various materials onto a surface

comedy.definition: a play or film intended to make the audience laugh

composition.definition: a piece of music; the way in which a whole is made up

contemporary.definition: living or occurring at the same time; belonging to the present

critique.definition: a detailed analysis and assessment; to review in detail

dialogue.definition: conversation between two or more people in a written work

epic.definition: a long narrative poem; heroic or grand in scale

fable.definition: a short story conveying a moral, often with animals as characters

fantasy.definition: the faculty of imagining impossible things; a genre of fiction

fiction.definition: literature describing imaginary events and people

folklore.definition: the traditional beliefs, customs, and stories of a community

fresco.definition: a painting done on wet plaster on a wall or ceiling

genre.definition: a category of artistic work with a particular form or style

haiku.definition: a Japanese poem of seventeen syllables in three lines

harmony.definition: the combination of simultaneously sounded musical notes

heritage.definition: property that is inherited; traditions passed down from predecessors

imagery.definition: visually descriptive language in a literary work

improvise.definition: to produce or perform something spontaneously; to make from available materials

irony.definition: expression of meaning through language signifying the opposite

legend.definition: a traditional story; an extremely famous person

lyric.definition: a short poem expressing personal feelings; relating to singing

metaphor.definition: a figure of speech applying a non-literal word to an object

modernism.definition: a movement breaking from classical and traditional forms

mosaic.definition: a picture made of small colored pieces; consisting of a variety

motif.definition: a distinctive feature recurring in an artistic work

mythology.definition: a collection of myths of a particular tradition

narrative.definition: a spoken or written account of connected events; a story

orchestra.definition: a large group of musicians playing various instruments

perspective.definition: a particular attitude or point of view; art technique showing depth

plot.definition: the main events of a story; a plan made secretly

prose.definition: written or spoken language in its ordinary form

protagonist.definition: the leading character in a story or drama

renaissance.definition: a revival of art and literature; the European cultural rebirth

rhythm.definition: a strong regular repeated pattern of sound or movement

satire.definition: the use of humor to expose and criticize people's stupidity

sculpture.definition: the art of making figures or objects in three dimensions

setting.definition: the place and time where a story takes place

silhouette.definition: the dark shape and outline of someone seen against a light background

soliloquy.definition: an act of speaking thoughts aloud when alone or regardless of hearers

sonnet.definition: a poem of fourteen lines with a specific rhyme scheme

surrealism.definition: a movement in art and literature to express the subconscious mind

symphony.definition: an elaborate musical composition for an orchestra

theme.definition: a subject of a talk or piece of writing; a recurring melody

tragedy.definition: an event causing great suffering; a serious drama with an unhappy ending

verse.definition: writing arranged with metrical rhythm; a paragraph of a song

adrenaline.definition: a hormone secreted in response to stress, preparing the body for action

antibiotic.definition: a medicine that inhibits the growth of or destroys microorganisms

artery.definition: a blood vessel carrying blood from the heart to the body

cholesterol.definition: a fatty substance found in blood; high levels can cause heart disease

cognitive.definition: relating to cognition; connected with the mental process of knowing

deficiency.definition: a lack or shortage of something essential

diagnose.definition: to identify the nature of an illness by examination of symptoms

diaphragm.definition: a dome-shaped muscle separating the chest from the abdomen

dna.definition: deoxyribonucleic acid; the molecule carrying genetic instructions

endorphin.definition: a hormone released to relieve pain and induce feelings of pleasure

epidemic.definition: a widespread occurrence of an infectious disease in a community

immune.definition: resistant to a particular infection; free from an obligation

inflammation.definition: a localized physical condition with heat, swelling, redness, and pain

lymph.definition: a colorless fluid containing white blood cells bathing the tissues

neurology.definition: the branch of medicine dealing with the nervous system

nutrition.definition: the process of providing food for health and growth

organ.definition: a part of an organism with a specific function

pandemic.definition: a disease prevalent over a whole country or the world

pathogen.definition: a bacterium, virus, or microorganism causing disease

physiology.definition: the branch of biology dealing with normal functions of organisms

placebo.definition: a substance with no pharmacological effect given as a control

prognosis.definition: the likely course of a medical condition; a forecast

quarantine.definition: isolation imposed to prevent spread of disease

respiratory.definition: relating to breathing or the organs used in breathing

syndrome.definition: a set of medical signs and symptoms correlated with each other

symptom.definition: a physical or mental sign indicating a condition of disease

vaccine.definition: a preparation administered to stimulate immunity to a disease

vein.definition: a blood vessel carrying blood toward the heart

virus.definition: an infective agent replicating only inside living cells

vitamin.definition: any of a group of organic compounds essential for normal growth

altruism.definition: unselfishness; concern for others' welfare over one's own

cognition.definition: the mental action of acquiring knowledge through thought

consciousness.definition: the state of being aware of and responsive to one's surroundings

deduction.definition: the inference of a conclusion from premises; logical reasoning

determinism.definition: the doctrine that events are determined by previously existing causes

dialectic.definition: the art of investigating truth by discussion; opposing forces

empiricism.definition: the theory that knowledge is derived only from experience

epistemology.definition: the branch of philosophy concerned with knowledge and belief

ethics.definition: moral principles that govern behavior; the branch studying them

existentialism.definition: the philosophy emphasizing individual existence and free choice

fallible.definition: capable of making mistakes; not perfect

free will.definition: the power to act without constraint; the ability to choose

hubris.definition: excessive pride or self-confidence leading to nemesis

humanism.definition: a rationalistic outlook valuing human beings and reason

identity.definition: the fact of being who one is; the characteristics defining a person

introspection.definition: the examination of one's own thoughts and feelings

karma.definition: the sum of actions in a life determining future life; destiny

logic.definition: reasoning conducted according to strict principles of validity

materialism.definition: a tendency to consider material possessions as important

meditation.definition: focusing the mind for relaxation or spiritual development

morality.definition: principles concerning the distinction between right and wrong

nihilism.definition: the rejection of all religious and moral principles; nothingness

perception.definition: the ability to see and understand; the way something is regarded

phenomenology.definition: the science of phenomena as distinct from science of being

pragmatism.definition: an approach evaluating theories by their practical consequences

psyche.definition: the human soul, mind, or spirit

psychology.definition: the scientific study of the human mind and its functions

rationalism.definition: the practice of treating reason as the basis of belief

stoicism.definition: enduring pain without complaint; an ancient school of philosophy

subconscious.definition: the part of the mind not fully conscious but influencing actions

utilitarianism.definition: the doctrine that actions are right if they benefit the majority

asynchronous.definition: not occurring at the same time; operations that do not block the caller

authentication.definition: the process of verifying the identity of a user or device

availability.definition: the degree to which a system is operational when needed

bottleneck.definition: a point of congestion causing the overall system to slow down

broadcast.definition: to transmit data to all devices in a network simultaneously

checksum.definition: a calculated value used to detect errors in transmitted data

client.definition: a computer or program that accesses services from a server

cluster.definition: a group of servers working together as a single system

concurrency.definition: the ability of different parts of a program to execute out of order

consistency.definition: ensuring all nodes in a distributed system have the same data

container.definition: a lightweight isolated environment for running applications

deadlock.definition: a situation where two threads are each waiting for the other to release a lock

deployment.definition: the process of releasing software to a production environment

distributed.definition: spread across multiple computers connected via a network

failover.definition: switching to a backup system when the primary system fails

firewall.definition: a network security system controlling incoming and outgoing traffic

gateway.definition: a network node serving as an entry point to another network

idempotent.definition: denoting an operation producing the same result however many times applied

load balancer.definition: a device distributing network traffic across multiple servers

microservice.definition: an architectural style structuring an application as small services

middleware.definition: software acting as a bridge between an operating system and applications

migration.definition: moving data or software from one system to another

multicast.definition: transmitting data to a specific group of destination computers

multithreading.definition: a technique allowing multiple threads to share a single CPU

network.definition: a group of interconnected computers sharing resources

node.definition: a point in a network; a single machine in a distributed system

packet.definition: a unit of data formatted for transmission over a network

partition.definition: a division of a database; separation of network nodes

payload.definition: the data carried in a transmission excluding headers

peer.definition: another computer at the same level in a network hierarchy

pipeline.definition: a chain of processing elements where the output feeds the next

port.definition: a communication endpoint identified by a number

proxy.definition: a server acting as an intermediary between clients and servers

queue.definition: a line of data items waiting to be processed; a first-in first-out structure

race condition.definition: a flaw where a system behaves unpredictably due to timing of operations

redundancy.definition: duplication of critical components to increase reliability

replication.definition: storing copies of data on multiple machines for reliability

scalability.definition: the ability of a system to handle growing amounts of work

semaphore.definition: a variable used to control access to a shared resource

server.definition: a computer providing data or services to other computers over a network

sharding.definition: partitioning a database so each part is stored on a different server

socket.definition: an endpoint for sending and receiving data across a network

synchronization.definition: coordinating simultaneous processes to avoid conflicts

throughput.definition: the amount of material processed in a given time period

timeout.definition: a cancellation of an operation due to it taking too long

token.definition: a symbol used to control access; a unit in a data stream

transaction.definition: a unit of work performed within a database management system

unicast.definition: transmission from one sender to one receiver over a network

uptime.definition: the time during which a machine is in operation

virtualization.definition: creating a virtual version of hardware, storage, or network

vulnerability.definition: a weakness in a system that can be exploited by an attacker

watchdog.definition: a timer resetting a system if software fails to respond in time

webhook.definition: a method of augmenting a web application with custom callbacks

workload.definition: the amount of work to be done by a machine or system
